title: Utility Layer — Infrastructure & Cross-Cutting Concerns

classes.name: DBUtil
methods: + provideConnection(): Connection [static] — lazy singleton; reads application.properties (db.connectionString, db.driverName, db.username, db.password); calls Class.forName(driverName) + DriverManager.getConnection()
+ closeConnection(rs: ResultSet): void [static] — closes ResultSet if open
+ closeConnection(ps: PreparedStatement): void [static] — closes PreparedStatement if open
classes.notes: Singleton connection pattern. Config read from 'application' ResourceBundle. closeConnection(Connection) is effectively a no-op.

classes.name: JavaMailUtil
methods: + sendMail(recipientMailId: String): void [static, public] — sends plain-text welcome email; hardcoded subject/body
# sendMail(recipient: String, subject: String, htmlTextMessage: String): void [static, protected] — sends HTML email; used by MailMessage
- prepareMessage(session: Session, myAccountEmail: String, recipientEmail: String): Message [static, private] — builds plain-text MimeMessage
- prepareMessage(session: Session, myAccountEmail: String, recipientEmail: String, subject: String, htmlTextMessage: String): Message [static, private] — builds HTML MimeMessage with setContent()
classes.notes: SMTP via smtp.gmail.com:587 with STARTTLS. Credentials from 'application' ResourceBundle (mailer.email, mailer.password). Overloaded sendMail and prepareMessage methods.

classes.name: MailMessage
methods: + registrationSuccess(emailId: String, name: String): void [static] — sends welcome HTML email with promo code ELLISON500
+ transactionSuccess(recipientEmail: String, name: String, transId: String, transAmount: double): void [static] — sends order confirmation HTML email
+ orderShipped(recipientEmail: String, name: String, transId: String, transAmount: double): void [static] — sends shipment notification HTML email
+ productAvailableNow(recipientEmail: String, name: String, prodName: String, prodId: String): void [static] — sends product availability HTML email
+ sendMessage(toEmailId: String, subject: String, htmlTextMessage: String): String [static] — generic send; returns 'SUCCESS' or 'FAILURE'
classes.notes: All methods delegate to JavaMailUtil.sendMail(recipient, subject, htmlTextMessage). Acts as a message template factory.

classes.name: IDUtil
methods: + generateId(): String [static] — returns 'P' + yyyyMMddhhmmss timestamp (product ID)
+ generateTransId(): String [static] — returns 'T' + yyyyMMddhhmmss timestamp (transaction ID)
classes.notes: Timestamp-based ID generation. Format: P20260417103045 / T20260417103045. No randomness — collision possible at same second.

classes.name: TestMail
methods: + main(args: String[]): void [static] — directly calls JavaMailUtil.sendMail(recipient, subject, htmlTextMessage); hardcoded recipient ellison.alumni@gmail.com
classes.notes: Standalone mail configuration smoke test. Not a JUnit test — uses main() entry point.

key_relationships: MailMessage depends_on JavaMailUtil (all 5 methods call JavaMailUtil.sendMail)
TestMail depends_on JavaMailUtil (main() calls JavaMailUtil.sendMail directly)
JavaMailUtil uses jakarta.mail API (Session, Transport, MimeMessage)
DBUtil uses java.sql API (DriverManager, Connection, PreparedStatement, ResultSet)
DBUtil reads application ResourceBundle for DB config
JavaMailUtil reads application ResourceBundle for SMTP config
IDUtil uses SimpleDateFormat + Date (no external dependencies)
shared_config_resource: application.properties (ResourceBundle) — used by both DBUtil and JavaMailUtil

method_visibility_summary.JavaMailUtil.sendMail(String,String,Stri: protected static — package-accessible to MailMessage and TestMail

important_notes: All classes are pure static utility classes (no instance state except DBUtil's static conn field). DBUtil implements a lazy singleton connection. MailMessage is the intended public API for sending business emails; JavaMailUtil is the low-level transport layer. IDUtil IDs are timestamp-based with no randomness.
